annotations.label: Quaternion

anchor.snippet: A quaternion is an expression of the form

annotations.note: `Quaternion R := ℍ[R,-1,0,-1]` defines quaternions over a ring, instantiated as `ℍ[R]`.

annotations.label: Zero quaternion

anchor.snippet: if a , b , c , d are all zero, the quaternion is the zero quaternion

annotations.note: The `Zero ℍ[R,c₁,c₂,c₃]` instance defines `0 = ⟨0,0,0,0⟩`.

annotations.label: Scalar and vector parts of a quaternion

anchor.snippet: can be decomposed into its scalar part

annotations.note: `QuaternionAlgebra.re` (the structure projection) and `QuaternionAlgebra.im` give the scalar and vector parts, with `re_add_im : ↑a.re + a.im = a`.

annotations.label: Scalar quaternion

anchor.snippet: A quaternion that equals its real part (that is, its vector part is zero) is called a scalar quaternion

annotations.note: There is no named `IsScalar`/`isReal` predicate, but `eq_re_iff_mem_range_coe` (and `star_eq_self`) characterize quaternions equal to their real part.

annotations.label: Vector quaternion

anchor.snippet: A quaternion that equals its vector part is called a vector quaternion

annotations.note: Mathlib lacks a named `IsVector` predicate, but characterizes vector quaternions via `star_eq_neg ↔ a.re = 0`.

annotations.label: Quaternions as a 4-dimensional vector space

anchor.snippet: Quaternions form a 4-dimensional vector space over the real numbers

annotations.note: `finrank_eq_four` and `rank_eq_four` show `Module.finrank R ℍ[R] = 4`, with explicit `basisOneIJK` basis.

annotations.label: Hamilton product

anchor.snippet: A multiplicative group structure, called the Hamilton product

annotations.note: The `Mul ℍ[R,c₁,c₂,c₃]` instance encodes the Hamilton product with explicit formula (`mk_mul_mk`).

annotations.label: Quaternions form a division algebra

anchor.snippet: Thus the quaternions form a division algebra

annotations.note: `instance instDivisionRing : DivisionRing ℍ[R]` (for linearly ordered field `R`) gives the division algebra structure; combined with `Algebra R ℍ[R]`.

annotations.label: Multiplication of basis elements by 1

anchor.snippet: The multiplication with 1 of the basis elements

annotations.note: Multiplication by `1` follows from the `One` and `Mul` instances and `mk_mul_mk` computes any product including those with `1`.

annotations.label: Products of basis elements i, j, k

anchor.snippet: The products of other basis elements are

annotations.note: The `QuaternionAlgebra.Basis` API contains the products `i_mul_k`, `k_mul_i`, `k_mul_j`, `j_mul_k`, `k_mul_k` (for the standard quaternion algebra these recover ij=k etc.).

annotations.label: Center of a noncommutative ring

anchor.snippet: The center of a noncommutative ring is the subring of elements

annotations.note: `Subring.center R` is the subring of elements commuting with every element of `R`.

annotations.label: Center of the quaternion algebra

anchor.snippet: The center of the quaternion algebra is the subfield of scalar quaternions

annotations.note: I could not locate a Mathlib theorem identifying `Subring.center ℍ[ℝ]` with the scalar quaternions.

annotations.label: Equation z^2 + 1 = 0 over quaternions

anchor.snippet: the equation z 2 + 1 = 0 , has infinitely many quaternion solutions

annotations.note: No theorem stating the equation `z^2+1=0` over `ℍ` has infinitely many solutions appears in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hamilton product expansion

anchor.snippet: their product, called the Hamilton product

annotations.note: `mk_mul_mk` (and `re_mul`, `imI_mul`, `imJ_mul`, `imK_mul` for `Quaternion`) expands the Hamilton product componentwise.

annotations.label: Scalar part and vector part

anchor.snippet: is called its scalar part

annotations.note: `re` (projection) and `im : ℍ[R,c₁,c₂,c₃] → ℍ[R,c₁,c₂,c₃]` define the scalar and vector parts.

annotations.label: Conjugate of a quaternion

anchor.snippet: The conjugate of q is the quaternion

annotations.note: Quaternion conjugation is the `Star` instance on `ℍ[R,c₁,c₂,c₃]`, with `imI_star`, `imJ_star`, `imK_star` giving the negation formulas.

annotations.label: Conjugation is an involution

anchor.snippet: Conjugation is an involution

annotations.note: Mathlib's `StarRing ℍ[R,c₁,c₂,c₃]` instance (`instStarRing`) makes conjugation an involution, so `star_star a = a`.

annotations.label: Conjugate of a product reverses order

anchor.snippet: The conjugate of a product of two quaternions is the product of the conjugates in the reverse order

annotations.note: Inherited from `StarRing ℍ[R,c₁,c₂,c₃]`: `star (a * b) = star b * star a`.

annotations.label: Conjugation expressible via quaternion arithmetic

anchor.snippet: The conjugation of a quaternion, in contrast to the complex setting, can be expressed with multiplication and addition of quaternions

annotations.note: `star_eq_two_re_sub : star a = ↑(2 * a.re) - a` gives conjugation by addition/multiplication only.

annotations.label: Extracting scalar and vector parts via conjugation

anchor.snippet: Conjugation can be used to extract the scalar and vector parts

annotations.note: `self_add_star : a + star a = 2 * a.re` (and `re_add_im`, `sub_re_self`) recover the scalar and vector parts via conjugation.

annotations.label: Norm of a quaternion

anchor.snippet: The square root of the product of a quaternion with its conjugate is called its norm

annotations.note: `normSq a = (a * star a).re`, and the `NormedAddCommGroup ℍ` instance in `Mathlib.Analysis.Quaternion` provides the norm itself.

annotations.label: Quaternion norm equals Euclidean norm on R^4

anchor.snippet: it is the same as the Euclidean norm

annotations.note: `linearIsometryEquivTuple : ℍ ≃ₗᵢ[ℝ] EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin 4)` is precisely the isometry with the Euclidean norm on `ℝ⁴`.

annotations.label: Norm scales by absolute value of real scalar

anchor.snippet: Multiplying a quaternion by a real number scales its norm by the absolute value of the number

annotations.note: The `NormedAlgebra ℝ ℍ` instance (and `Quaternion.norm_coe`) makes scalar multiplication norm-multiplicative: `‖r • q‖ = |r| * ‖q‖`.

annotations.label: Norm is multiplicative

anchor.snippet: the norm is multiplicative

annotations.note: The `NormedDivisionRing ℍ` instance bundles `norm_mul a b = ‖a‖ * ‖b‖`; `normSq` is also a `MonoidWithZeroHom`.

annotations.label: Distance between quaternions

anchor.snippet: to define the distance

annotations.note: Distance `dist a b = ‖a - b‖` follows from the `NormedAddCommGroup ℍ` instance.

annotations.label: Quaternions form a metric space

anchor.snippet: a metric space

annotations.note: `NormedAddCommGroup ℍ` (plus `CompleteSpace ℍ`) endows quaternions with a metric/complete metric space structure.

annotations.label: Unit quaternion and versor

anchor.snippet: A unit quaternion is a quaternion of norm one

annotations.note: Unit quaternions are not a named subgroup, but can be expressed via `unitary ℍ` or `Metric.sphere (0:ℍ) 1`; no dedicated `UnitQuaternion`/`versor` declaration exists.

annotations.label: Polar decomposition of nonzero quaternion

anchor.snippet: Every nonzero quaternion has a unique polar decomposition

annotations.note: Mathlib has no explicit theorem decomposing a nonzero quaternion uniquely as norm × unit quaternion.

annotations.label: Reciprocal of a quaternion

anchor.snippet: So the reciprocal of q is defined to be

annotations.note: `instInv : Inv ℍ[R]` defines `a⁻¹ = (normSq a)⁻¹ • star a` (via `instGroupWithZero`/`instDivisionRing`).

annotations.label: Quaternions form a non-commutative associative algebra

anchor.snippet: are a non-commutative, associative algebra over the real numbers

annotations.note: `Algebra R ℍ[R]` plus `Ring ℍ[R]` give the associative algebra structure; non-commutativity is implicit in the multiplication formula.

annotations.label: Frobenius theorem

anchor.snippet: The Frobenius theorem states that there are exactly three

annotations.note: Mathlib does not contain Frobenius's classification of finite-dimensional associative real division algebras.

annotations.label: Hurwitz's theorem

anchor.snippet: Hurwitz's theorem says that there are only four

annotations.note: Hurwitz's classification of normed division algebras over ℝ (ℝ, ℂ, ℍ, 𝕆) is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Quaternion group Q_8

anchor.snippet: This non-abelian group is called the quaternion group

annotations.note: `QuaternionGroup n` is the generalized quaternion group of order `4n`; `QuaternionGroup 2` is Q₈.

annotations.label: Quaternions as quotient of real group ring of Q_8

anchor.snippet: The quaternions are isomorphic to the quotient ring

annotations.note: No isomorphism `ℝ[Q₈]/⟨1+x⟩ ≃ ℍ` appears in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Dot product of vector quaternions

anchor.snippet: their dot product

annotations.note: The inner product `⟪a,b⟫ = (a * star b).re` from the `InnerProductSpace ℝ ℍ` instance specializes to the 3D dot product on vector quaternions, but there is no dedicated `dot` for them.

annotations.label: Cross product of vector quaternions

anchor.snippet: The cross product of p and q relative to the orientation

annotations.note: `Matrix.crossProduct` exists on `Fin 3 → R` but there is no formal identification of it with the vector part of `p*q` on vector quaternions.

annotations.label: Commutator of vector quaternions

anchor.snippet: For the commutator

annotations.note: There is no Mathlib lemma identifying `p*q - q*p` of vector quaternions with twice the cross product.

annotations.label: Commutativity criterion via collinearity

anchor.snippet: two quaternions commute if and only if their vector parts are collinear

annotations.note: Mathlib has no theorem characterizing commuting quaternions by collinear vector parts.

annotations.label: Unit quaternions identified with rotations

anchor.snippet: Unit quaternions can be identified with rotations

annotations.note: No Mathlib declaration realizes the map from unit quaternions to SO(3) rotations.

annotations.label: Representation as complex 2x2 matrices

anchor.snippet: can be represented using a complex 2 × 2 matrix as

annotations.note: No ring homomorphism `ℍ →* Matrix (Fin 2) (Fin 2) ℂ` realizing the standard 2×2 representation is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Properties of complex 2x2 representation

anchor.snippet: This representation has the following properties

annotations.note: No properties (determinant=normSq, trace=2re, etc.) of a quaternion-to-2×2-complex representation appear in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Representation as real 4x4 matrices

anchor.snippet: Using 4 × 4 real matrices, that same quaternion can be written as

annotations.note: No quaternion-to-4×4-real representation is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: 48 distinct real 4x4 representations

anchor.snippet: There are 48 distinct matrix representations of this form

annotations.note: No combinatorial statement about 48 such representations is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Lagrange's four-square theorem

anchor.snippet: every nonnegative integer is the sum of four integer squares

annotations.note: `Nat.sum_four_squares n : ∃ a b c d, a^2 + b^2 + c^2 + d^2 = n` is Lagrange's four-square theorem.

annotations.label: Quaternions via Cayley–Dickson construction

anchor.section: Quaternions as pairs of complex numbers
anchor.snippet: quaternions are the result of applying the Cayley–Dickson construction to the complex numbers

annotations.note: The Cayley–Dickson construction is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Square roots of -1 form the unit sphere

anchor.snippet: A quaternion squares to −1 if and only if it is a vector quaternion with norm 1

annotations.note: I could not find a Mathlib theorem characterizing `{q : ℍ | q^2 = -1}` as the unit 2-sphere in vector quaternions.

annotations.label: Only negative real quaternions have infinitely many square roots

anchor.snippet: Only negative real quaternions have infinitely many square roots

annotations.note: No statement counting square roots of quaternions appears in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Complex plane embedded via square root of -1

anchor.section: As a union of complex planes
anchor.snippet: This is an injective ring homomorphism

annotations.note: `Quaternion.ofComplex : ℂ →ₐ[ℝ] ℍ` is the canonical embedding using `i`; a general statement that any square root of `-1` induces an embedding is not formalized.

annotations.label: Subalgebra generated by non-real quaternion

anchor.section: As a union of complex planes
anchor.snippet: Every non-real quaternion generates a subalgebra of the quaternions that is isomorphic to

annotations.note: No theorem identifying the subalgebra generated by a non-real quaternion with `ℂ` is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Commutative subrings of the quaternion ring

anchor.snippet: two quaternions p and q commute

annotations.note: No description of commutative subrings of ℍ in terms of collinear vector parts is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Existence of square roots of arbitrary quaternions

anchor.snippet: has at least one square root

annotations.note: Mathlib has no `∃ r, r^2 = q` statement for arbitrary quaternions.

annotations.label: Number of square roots by quaternion type

anchor.snippet: nonzero, non-scalar quaternions, or positive scalar quaternions, have exactly two roots

annotations.note: No case analysis of square-root multiplicity by quaternion type is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Exponential of a quaternion

anchor.snippet: the exponential is computed as

annotations.note: `NormedSpace.exp ℝ : ℍ → ℍ` is available and `Quaternion.exp_eq` gives the closed-form `exp q = exp q.re • (cos‖q.im‖ + (sin‖q.im‖/‖q.im‖) • q.im)`.

annotations.label: Logarithm of a quaternion

anchor.snippet: and the logarithm is

annotations.note: There is no `Quaternion.log` definition in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Polar decomposition of a quaternion

anchor.snippet: the polar decomposition of a quaternion may be written

annotations.note: The polar form `q = ‖q‖ exp(θ n̂)` is implicit via `exp_eq` plus norm/im decomposition but not stated as its own decomposition theorem.

annotations.label: Polar form of a unit quaternion

anchor.snippet: Any unit quaternion may be expressed in polar form as

annotations.note: `exp_of_re_eq_zero` gives `exp(n̂ θ) = cos θ + sin θ · n̂` for imaginary `q`, the polar form of a unit quaternion, though no `polarForm` definition is named.

annotations.label: Power of a quaternion

anchor.snippet: The power of a quaternion raised to an arbitrary (real) exponent

annotations.note: Real (non-integer) powers of quaternions are not defined in Mathlib (only `Monoid.npow`/`zpow`).

annotations.label: Geodesic distance between unit quaternions

anchor.snippet: The geodesic distance

annotations.note: No geodesic distance on the unit-quaternion 3-sphere is defined in the Mathlib quaternion files.

annotations.label: Conjugation by unit quaternion as rotation

anchor.snippet: Conjugation by a unit quaternion

annotations.note: No theorem that `v ↦ q * v * q⁻¹` is a rotation of ℝ³ is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Unit quaternions double cover SO(3)

anchor.snippet: The set of all unit quaternions ( versors ) forms a 3-sphere

annotations.note: The double cover `Spin(3) → SO(3)` via unit quaternions is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Versors' group isomorphic to SU(2)

anchor.snippet: The versors' group is isomorphic to SU(2)

annotations.note: No isomorphism between unit quaternions and SU(2) is formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Ring of Hurwitz quaternions

anchor.snippet: is called the ring of Hurwitz quaternions

annotations.note: The Hurwitz integer ring is absent from Mathlib (the only `Hurwitz` hits are zeta-function lemmas).

annotations.label: 24 unit Hurwitz quaternions form the 24-cell

anchor.snippet: There are 24 unit quaternions in this ring, and they are the vertices of a regular 24 cell

annotations.note: Neither Hurwitz quaternions nor the 24-cell polytope are in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Quaternion algebra over a field

anchor.snippet: a four-dimensional unitary associative algebra can be defined over F with basis

annotations.note: `QuaternionAlgebra R a b c` defines the generalized quaternion algebra over any ring `R`, including any field.

annotations.label: Classification of quaternion algebras

anchor.snippet: Quaternion algebras are isomorphic to the algebra of 2×2 matrices

annotations.note: Classification of quaternion algebras over a field as either a division algebra or `M₂(F)` is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Reflection formula in Cl_{3,0}

anchor.section: Quaternions as the even part of Cl 3,0 (ℝ)
anchor.snippet: the reflection of a vector r in a plane perpendicular to a unit vector w can be written

annotations.note: The Clifford-algebra reflection identity `r' = -w r w` for `Cl₃,₀(ℝ)` is not formalized.

annotations.label: Isomorphism between quaternions and even Cl_{3,0}

anchor.section: Quaternions as the even part of Cl 3,0 (ℝ)
anchor.snippet: the two structures

annotations.note: No isomorphism `ℍ ≃ₐ[ℝ] CliffordAlgebra⁺ (Q₃,₀)` appears in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Brauer group of the reals

anchor.snippet: the Brauer group of the real numbers consists of two classes

annotations.note: The `BrauerGroup` is defined in `Mathlib.Algebra.BrauerGroup.Defs`, but `Br(ℝ) ≃ ℤ/2` is not proved.

annotations.label: Artin–Wedderburn theorem (Wedderburn's part)

anchor.snippet: By the Artin–Wedderburn theorem

annotations.note: `isSemisimpleRing_iff_pi_matrix_divisionRing` (and `exists_ringEquiv_matrix_divisionRing` for simple Artinian rings) is the Artin–Wedderburn structure theorem.
